skill.name: domain-name-brainstormer
skill.description: Generates creative domain name ideas for projects, brands, and products, with guidance for checking availability across common TLDs.
skill.summary: Brainstorm brandable domain names and compare extension options.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby shell backtick execution, weak cryptographic algorithms, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown prompt examples, domain extension references, and naming advice, with no executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fenced Examples Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The detected backtick locations are Markdown code fences containing user prompt examples and sample output. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding SKILL.md content shows triple-backtick Markdown examples, not executable Ruby or script files. No command invocation or user-input shell interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Domain Naming Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive domain naming content, TLD examples, and sample domain suggestions. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic operation is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language Markdown and domain examples such as TLD guidance and sample names. There is no code path that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: Naming Advice Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance detections are ordinary domain naming advice about pronounceability and avoiding numbers. They do not collect host, user, process, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are checklist advice for choosing readable names. No reconnaissance command, filesystem access, environment access, or network probing appears in the skill.

content.value_statement: Finding a memorable domain can take hours of manual naming and availability checks. This skill helps generate focused name ideas, compare TLD options, and choose practical next steps.
actual_capabilities: Generates domain name ideas from a project, product, or brand description.
Suggests options across common TLDs such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, and .co.
Explains why each suggested name may fit the audience or product category.
Creates variations based on preferred keywords, languages, length, or tone.
Provides practical selection guidance, including memorability, spelling, and next registration steps.
limitations: Availability checks depend on the assistant or connected tools used during the session.
It does not replace a registrar, trademark attorney, or formal brand clearance search.
Pricing estimates for premium domains can change quickly and require verification.
Social handle availability may need separate platform-specific checks.

use_cases.title: Name a Startup Launch
use_cases.description: Generate short, brandable domain options for a new company and compare practical TLD choices.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Domain
use_cases.description: Explore names that match a product audience, feature set, and preferred positioning.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Brand
use_cases.description: Find domain variations for a portfolio, consulting practice, newsletter, or creator project.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Domain Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a [project type] for [target audience]. Suggest memorable domain names and explain the best options.

prompt_templates.title: TLD-Focused Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest domain names for [product]. Prioritize short names across .com, .io, .dev, and .ai.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword-Constrained Naming
prompt_templates.prompt: Brainstorm domain names for [business]. Use or reference these keywords: [keywords]. Avoid hyphens and numbers.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Naming Pattern Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review naming patterns used by [industry] products, then suggest available-style domain ideas for [project] with pros and cons.

output_examples.input: A developer tool for sharing reusable code snippets.
output_examples.output: The skill returns names such as concise developer-focused options, explains why each fits, groups choices by TLD, and recommends a top pick.

output_examples.input: A sustainable fashion brand that wants an eco-focused domain.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests names using sustainability and fashion cues, compares .com alternatives with niche TLDs, and notes which names are easier to say.

output_examples.input: A personal portfolio for a designer named Maya Chen.
output_examples.output: The skill creates name variations using the person name, profession, and portfolio intent, then recommends options for consistent branding.

best_practices: Provide the audience, product category, tone, and any words to include or avoid.
Ask for a shortlist with reasoning before checking every possible variation.
Verify final availability, trademark conflicts, and social handles before purchasing.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on generated availability claims without checking a registrar.
Do not choose names that are hard to spell, pronounce, or share verbally.
Do not skip legal review when the domain will represent a commercial brand.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee that a domain is available?
faq.answer: No. It can guide availability checks, but final confirmation should happen through a registrar or trusted domain lookup tool.

faq.question: Which TLDs does it consider?
faq.answer: It mentions common options such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, .xyz, .design, and .tech.

faq.question: Can it help with non-English names?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes multilingual brainstorming and can generate names based on language preferences.

faq.question: Does it check trademarks?
faq.answer: It reminds users to check trademarks, but it does not provide legal clearance or legal advice.

faq.question: Is it useful if my first domain choice is taken?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create variations, alternative TLD choices, and related names that keep the same brand direction.

faq.question: Does the skill run code or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is a Markdown instruction file with examples and naming guidance only.
